creator: wgodfrey@ena.com
text:
Thanks, let me know if you need anything.  The exported filesystem is
ZFS.  Should I open a new ticket?

Weldon

-----Original Message-----
From: Kris Kennaway [mailto:kris@FreeBSD.org]=20
Sent: Friday, July 11, 2008 3:34 PM
To: Weldon Godfrey; freebsd-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.org
Subject: Re: kern/124280: [nfs] [panic] FreeBSD 7/amd64 STABLE crash

Weldon Godfrey wrote:
> I think so (forgive me, it has been a long long time since I delt with
a
> debugger).  Please let me know if you need anything else or something
> different :)
>=20
> kgdb) backtrace
> #0  doadump () at pcpu.h:194
> #1  0x0000000000000004 in ?? ()
> #2  0xffffffff80488339 in boot (howto=3D260) at
> /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_shutdown.c:418
> #3  0xffffffff8048873d in panic (fmt=3D0x104 <Address 0x104 out of
> bounds>)
>     at /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_shutdown.c:572
> #4  0xffffffff80747f94 in trap_fatal (frame=3D0xffffff000c27a6a0,
> eva=3D18446742974380717160)
>     at /usr/src/sys/amd64/amd64/trap.c:724
> #5  0xffffffff80748365 in trap_pfault (frame=3D0xffffffffdfbc9670,
> usermode=3D0)
>     at /usr/src/sys/amd64/amd64/trap.c:641
> #6  0xffffffff80748ca8 in trap (frame=3D0xffffffffdfbc9670) at
> /usr/src/sys/amd64/amd64/trap.c:410
> #7  0xffffffff8072e77e in calltrap () at
> /usr/src/sys/amd64/amd64/exception.S:169
> #8  0xffffffff80620008 in nfsrv_access (vp=3D0xffffff00a3a323e0,
> flags=3D128, cred=3D0xffffff009a2a3e00,=20
>     rdonly=3D0, td=3D0xffffff000c27a6a0, override=3D0) at
> /usr/src/sys/nfsserver/nfs_serv.c:4284
> #9  0xffffffff80621121 in nfsrv3_access (nfsd=3D0xffffff000c152500,
> slp=3D0xffffff000a02b100,=20
>     td=3D0xffffff000c27a6a0, mrq=3D0xffffffffdfbc9af0) at
> /usr/src/sys/nfsserver/nfs_serv.c:234
> #10 0xffffffff8063195d in nfssvc (td=3DVariable "td" is not available.
> ) at /usr/src/sys/nfsserver/nfs_syscalls.c:461
> #11 0xffffffff807485e7 in syscall (frame=3D0xffffffffdfbc9c70) at
> /usr/src/sys/amd64/amd64/trap.c:852
> #12 0xffffffff8072e98b in Xfast_syscall () at
> /usr/src/sys/amd64/amd64/exception.S:290
> #13 0x00000008006877bc in ?? ()

Thanks, looks like it's a different bug that just happens to also=20
involve NFS.

Kris
